task_objective: Capture the Steam Deck's current screen, then package the working method as a reusable helper script.
design_decisions: The Deck runs a gamescope session: grim is not installed and spectacle cannot attach to the compositor, so gamescopectl screenshot is the only working capture path.
SSH lands in a tty session, so the capture command must set XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000 to reach the graphical session.
gamescopectl returns exit 0 before the PNG exists; the script polls stat until the size is non-zero and stable across two samples, so a partially written file is never copied.
Bash rather than Python, invoked directly instead of through ./run.sh, so the helper works without the project virtualenv.
Tests inject stub ssh/scp binaries on PATH with a mapped remote root, so the suite needs no Deck and passes on CI.
results: Verified live against the Deck (1280x800 RGBA PNG returned). ruff check, ruff format, and ty check pass; full suite 848 passed at 88.69% coverage.
